title: Trump Reviews New Iranian Peace Proposal But Expresses Deep Skepticism
summary: President Trump confirmed reviewing a new Iranian proposal to end the war but stated he 'can't imagine it meet his standards,' expressing frustration with Iran's 'disjointed leadership' and questioning whether to 'blast them' or negotiate.
contents:
## Trump Doubts Latest Iran Peace Proposal

### Reviewing the Terms
President Donald Trump confirmed on May 2 that he had received a new Iranian proposal to end the war, telling reporters "I'll let you know about it later" and noting officials would provide him with "the exact wording." Iran's state-run IRNA news agency reported Tehran handed the plan to mediators in Pakistan on Thursday night.

### Dismissive Response
Shortly after his initial comments, Trump posted on Truth Social that he "can't imagine that it meet his standards in that they have not yet paid a big enough price for what they have done to Humanity, and the World, over the last 47 years." This statement follows his rejection of a previous Iranian proposal earlier in the week.

### Frustration with Iran Leadership
Trump voiced frustration with Iran's leadership structure: "They're asking for things that I can't agree to" and described them as "a very disjointed leadership" where "they all want to make a deal, but they're all messed up." He previously posed a stark choice between military escalation and negotiation.

### War Status
The fragile three-week ceasefire between the US and Iran continues to hold amid growing economic pressure. Both countries traded accusations of violations while diplomatic channels remain active through Pakistani mediation.

**Sources:** DW News, Fortune, AP News
